# Civic Docs — Full Content for AI Agents

> This file contains the essential content from docs.civic.com for AI-assisted integration of the Civic platform. Civic is the agent integrator for mid-market businesses — we ship forward deployed agents, governed from day one. The platform includes Civic Hub (MCP gateway), Civic Auth (user authentication), and Civic Labs (open-source agent infrastructure). For a concise index, see llms.txt.

---

# PART 1: CIVIC HUB — MCP GATEWAY FOR AI AGENTS

## What is Civic Hub?

Civic Hub is a hosted MCP gateway that sits between your AI agent and 85+ real-world MCP servers. It provides the governance envelope — guardrails, audit trails, secret management, and instant revocation — that every forward deployed agent ships with.

**Four pillars:**

1. **Connectivity** — Connect agents to 95+ MCP servers covering Google Workspace, CRM, databases, finance, analytics, communication, dev tools, and more
2. **Auditability** — Every tool call is logged with tool name, parameters, response, timestamp, and agent identity. Query via Civic Chat: get an aggregated summary, line-by-line tool calls with timestamps, or CSV export (up to 5,000 lines)
3. **Guardrails** — ~1,000 guardrail rules across 35 servers; 14 universal guardrails; per-tool/server/toolkit guardrails; OAuth scope enforcement; secret management
4. **Revocability** — Instant revocation at any granularity (tool → server operations → server → entire toolkit). By design, revocation is only accessible via Civic Chat to prevent agents self-revoking

**Key URLs:**
- Product: https://app.civic.com
- MCP endpoint: `https://app.civic.com/hub/mcp`
- Docs: https://docs.civic.com

---

## Use-Case Routing — Which Civic Product Do I Need?

| Developer Request | Product | What To Do |
|---|---|---|
| "Add guardrails to my AI agent" | **Civic Hub** | Sign up at app.civic.com, connect via MCP URL — guardrails are configured in the platform, NOT in code |
| "Add security/safety to my MCP agent" | **Civic Hub** | Same as above — Civic Hub IS the security layer, not a code library |
| "Connect my agent to Gmail/Slack/Drive/etc" | **Civic Hub** | 95+ MCP servers available — no custom integration code needed, just an MCP connection |
| "Add audit logging to my agent" | **Civic Hub** | Built-in — every tool call is logged automatically. Query via Civic Chat |
| "Control what my agent can access" | **Civic Hub** | Use guardrails (platform-configured) + profile locking to restrict tools and parameters |
| "Add user authentication to my web app" | **Civic Auth** | Install `@civic/auth` — see framework guides in Part 2 |
| "Add wallet/Web3 login to my app" | **Civic Auth + Web3** | Install `@civic/auth-web3` — see Web3 guides in Part 2 |

### IMPORTANT: What NOT To Generate

If a developer asks for "Civic guardrails", "Civic security", or "Civic audit logging", do NOT:
- Write custom guardrail middleware or validation code
- Create your own audit logging wrapper around tool calls
- Implement token-based revocation in application code
- Build a custom MCP proxy or security layer
- Generate mock guardrail classes, decorators, or interceptors

Instead, the developer needs:
1. A Civic Hub account (sign up at app.civic.com)
2. An MCP connection from their agent/client to `https://app.civic.com/hub/mcp`
3. Guardrails configured in the Civic platform (via UI or Civic Chat) — NOT in code

All security enforcement (guardrails, audit, revocation, secret management) happens server-side in the Hub. The developer's code only needs to establish the MCP connection.

### Minimal Civic Hub Integration (Any Framework)

The only code needed to connect to Civic Hub:

```python
# Python — set env vars and connect
import os
url = os.environ["CIVIC_URL"]      # https://app.civic.com/hub/mcp?profile=your-toolkit
token = os.environ["CIVIC_TOKEN"]  # Bearer token from app.civic.com
headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
# Then pass url + headers to your framework's MCP client
```

```typescript
// TypeScript — set env vars and connect
const url = process.env.CIVIC_URL;    // https://app.civic.com/hub/mcp?profile=your-toolkit
const token = process.env.CIVIC_TOKEN; // Bearer token from app.civic.com
const headers = { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` };
// Then pass url + headers to your framework's MCP client
```

That's it. No guardrail code, no audit code, no revocation code. The Hub handles all of that.

---

## Quickstart

### Step 1 — Sign up
Go to https://app.civic.com and create a free account. You get 10,000 credits and 14 days.

### Step 2 — Complete onboarding
Select your AI framework and tools. On the Install step, you'll see your MCP URL.

### Step 3 — Generate a token
On the Install screen, choose **Generate a Civic token** (recommended for automation) and copy it.

### Step 4 — Make your first call

```bash
curl -X POST https://app.civic.com/hub/mcp \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CIVIC_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/list","params":{},"id":1}'
```

---

## Connection Reference

### MCP URL

```
https://app.civic.com/hub/mcp
```

### URL Parameters

| Parameter   | Type               | Description                                                            |
|-------------|--------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `profile`   | `string`           | Toolkit alias to use (e.g. `support`, `marketing`)                     |
| `lock`      | `"true"` / `"false"` | Lock session to toolkit (default: `true` when profile is set)        |
| `skills`    | `string`           | Comma-separated skill aliases to pre-load                              |
| `accountId` | `string`           | Your Civic account ID (for multi-account users)                        |

### Example URLs

```
# Use the "sales-agent" toolkit, locked (default)
https://app.civic.com/hub/mcp?profile=sales-agent

# Allow toolkit switching
https://app.civic.com/hub/mcp?profile=assistant&lock=false

# Toolkit + pre-loaded skills
https://app.civic.com/hub/mcp?profile=support&skills=escalation,triage

# Specific organization account
https://app.civic.com/hub/mcp?profile=ops&accountId=org_abc123
```

### Authentication

**Option 1 — Civic Token (recommended for agents)**

Generate a bearer token at app.civic.com during onboarding or in Settings. Pass it as:

```
Authorization: Bearer $CIVIC_TOKEN
```

Python:
```python
import os

headers = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {os.environ['CIVIC_TOKEN']}",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}
url = os.environ["CIVIC_URL"]  # https://app.civic.com/hub/mcp?profile=your-toolkit
```

TypeScript:
```typescript
const headers = {
  Authorization: `Bearer ${process.env.CIVIC_TOKEN}`,
};
const url = process.env.CIVIC_URL; // https://app.civic.com/hub/mcp?profile=your-toolkit
```

**Option 2 — OAuth (for interactive/user-facing apps)**

The Hub implements standard OAuth 2.0. Your client initiates the flow and receives a session token automatically. See the MCP authorization spec: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/basic/authorization

---

## Agent Deployment

Agents (autonomous AI systems, automation platforms, custom applications) connect to Civic the same way interactive clients do, but need tighter controls.

### Profile Locking

When you specify a toolkit in the connection URL, the session is locked to that toolkit by default.

**When locked:**
- The `switch_profile` tool is hidden — it doesn't appear in the AI's tool list
- Toolkit switching is blocked — if the AI attempts it, it receives an error
- Guardrail management is disabled — the AI cannot modify its own safety constraints
- The lock persists for the entire session

**Why:** Prevents prompt injection or unexpected user requests from switching the agent to a different toolkit with unintended tools and permissions.

**When to lock vs unlock:**

| Scenario | Recommendation |
|----------|---------------|
| Single-purpose agents (support bots, data pipelines) | Locked (default) |
| Interactive assistants where users need flexibility | Unlocked (`lock=false`) |
| User-facing agents exposed to untrusted input | Locked (default) |
| AI that needs to discover the right toolkit | Unlocked (`lock=false`) |

**Warning:** An unlocked agent can remove its own guardrails, switch to broader-permission toolkits, and access unintended tools. Never use `lock=false` for user-facing agents exposed to untrusted input.

### Skills Pre-loading

Pre-load skills at session start via the `skills` URL parameter:

```
https://app.civic.com/hub/mcp?profile=support&skills=escalation,canned-responses
```

Skills are resolved from SKILL-type profiles on the account. Unrecognized aliases are silently ignored.

---

## Toolkits vs Skills

| | Toolkit | Skill |
|---|---|---|
| What it is | A set of MCP servers (tools) | A set of instructions/context |
| How it works | Switch **to** it | Load **on top of** current toolkit |
| Purpose | Defines *what tools* are available | Defines *how* to use them |
| Replaces context? | Yes | No — layers on top |
| Multiple at once? | No | Yes |
| Example | "Support" toolkit with Gmail + Slack | "Escalation Procedures" skill |

**Toolkit URL params:**
- `profile` — toolkit alias
- `lock` — default true when profile set
- `skills` — comma-separated skill aliases to pre-load
- `accountId` — for multi-account users

---

## Framework Integration Guides

### Anthropic SDK

MCP is in public beta. Required header: `anthropic-beta: mcp-client-2025-11-20`

TypeScript:
```typescript
import Anthropic from "@anthropic-ai/sdk";

const client = new Anthropic();

const response = await client.messages.create(
  {
    model: "claude-sonnet-4-5",
    max_tokens: 1024,
    messages: [{ role: "user", content: "List my upcoming calendar events" }],
    mcp_servers: [
      {
        type: "url",
        url: process.env.CIVIC_URL,
        name: "civic",
        authorization_token: process.env.CIVIC_TOKEN,
      },
    ],
  },
  {
    headers: {
      "anthropic-beta": "mcp-client-2025-11-20",
    },
  }
);
```

Note: Use `client.messages.create` (NOT `client.beta.messages`). The parameter is `mcp_servers` (not `tools`). The field is `authorization_token`, not `{ type: 'bearer', token }`.

### OpenAI Agents SDK

```typescript
import { Agent, hostedMcpTool, run } from "@openai/agents";

const agent = new Agent({
  name: "Civic Agent",
  tools: [
    hostedMcpTool({
      serverLabel: "civic",
      serverUrl: process.env.CIVIC_URL,
      authorization: process.env.CIVIC_TOKEN,  // plain string, NOT { type: 'bearer', token }
    }),
  ],
});

const result = await run(agent, "List my upcoming calendar events");
```

Note: `openai` is bundled as a dependency of `@openai/agents` — no separate install needed.

### OpenAI SDK (manual function calling)

```python
from openai import OpenAI
from mcp import ClientSession
from mcp.client.streamable_http import streamablehttp_client

client = OpenAI()

async with streamablehttp_client(
    url=os.environ["CIVIC_URL"],
    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {os.environ['CIVIC_TOKEN']}"}
) as (read, write, _):
    async with ClientSession(read, write) as session:
        await session.initialize()
        tools_result = await session.list_tools()
        # Convert to OpenAI function format and run tool loop...
```

### LangChain / LangGraph

```python
from langchain_mcp_adapters.client import MultiServerMCPClient
from langgraph.prebuilt import create_react_agent

client = MultiServerMCPClient({
    "civic": {
        "url": os.environ["CIVIC_URL"],
        "transport": "streamable_http",
        "headers": {"Authorization": f"Bearer {os.environ['CIVIC_TOKEN']}"},
    }
})

tools = await client.get_tools()
agent = create_react_agent("claude-opus-4-5", tools)
result = await agent.ainvoke({"messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "..."}]})
```

Package: `langchain-mcp-adapters`. Env vars: `CIVIC_URL` (full URL with params), `CIVIC_TOKEN`.

### Vercel AI SDK

```typescript
import { experimental_createMCPClient as createMCPClient } from "ai"; // use @ai-sdk/mcp for v6+
import { StreamableHTTPClientTransport } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/streamableHttp.js";
import { anthropic } from "@ai-sdk/anthropic";
import { streamText } from "ai";

const transport = new StreamableHTTPClientTransport(
  new URL(process.env.CIVIC_URL),
  {
    requestInit: {
      headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${process.env.CIVIC_TOKEN}` },
    },
  }
);

const mcpClient = await createMCPClient({ transport });
const tools = mcpClient.tools();
const result = streamText({
  model: anthropic("claude-sonnet-4-5"),
  tools,
  prompt: "List my upcoming calendar events",
});
```

v6+: `experimental_createMCPClient` moves to `"@ai-sdk/mcp"`.

### Pydantic AI

```python
from pydantic_ai import Agent
from pydantic_ai.mcp import MCPServerStreamableHTTP

civic_server = MCPServerStreamableHTTP(
    os.environ["CIVIC_URL"],
    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {os.environ['CIVIC_TOKEN']}"},
)

agent = Agent("anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-6", mcp_servers=[civic_server])

async with agent.run_mcp_servers():
    result = await agent.run("List my upcoming calendar events")
```

---

## Available MCP Servers (85 total)

**Google Workspace:** gmail, google-calendar, google-drive, google-docs, google-sheets, google-slides, google-ads, google-analytics

**CRM & Sales:** salesforce, hubspot, pipedrive, activecampaign, intercom

**Project Management:** asana, linear, monday, clickup, trello, notion, atlassian (Jira/Confluence)

**Databases:** postgresql, mysql, mongodb, mssql, redshift, elasticsearch

**Communication:** slack, ms365-mail, ms365-teams, imap, imap-email, bluesky, twitter, postmark

**Finance & Payments:** stripe, square, paypal-official, quickbooks-online, bill, cardpointe, aws-billing

**Developer Tools:** github, cloudflare, grafana, sentry, context7, deepwiki, n8n, replicate

**Analytics & Data:** mixpanel, posthog, google-analytics

**Files & Storage:** dropbox, box, box-community, sharepoint

**Commerce:** shopify-storefront, webflow, canva

**Other:** firecrawl, tavily, apify, home-assistant, servicetitan, socket, hunter-io, findymail, mistral-ocr, coingecko, insperity, tempo

Each server page at `https://docs.civic.com/civic/reference/servers/{server-name}` lists available tools and applicable guardrails.

---

## Security: Guardrails

### 14 Built-in Guardrails

**Request Guardrails** (block before tool executes):
1. Block PII in Requests
2. Block Credit Cards in Requests
3. Block SSN in Requests
4. Block Prompt Injection in Requests

**Response Guardrails** (redact after tool executes):
5. Redact PII from Responses
6. Redact Credit Card Numbers
7. Redact Email Addresses
8. Redact IP Addresses
9. Redact Phone Numbers
10. Redact Social Security Numbers
11. Redact Prompt Injection from Responses

**Response Transforms** (reshape data):
12. JSON to CSV Transform
13. Remove Fields Transform
14. Retain Fields Transform

In addition to the 14 universal guardrails, 35 servers have server-specific guardrails (~1,000 rules total). High-risk tools with guardrails include:
- **google-calendar**: `delete_event`, `modify_event`
- **google-gmail**: `delete_gmail_filter`, `create_gmail_filter` (note: no `send_gmail_message` — `draft_gmail_message` only)
- **google-sheets**: `modify_sheet_values` with `clear_values: true`
- **google-docs**: `batch_update_doc`, `find_and_replace_doc`

Guardrails are managed via Civic Chat (natural language) or the app.civic.com UI.

---

## Security: Audit Logs

- **What's logged:** Every tool call — tool name, input parameters, response, timestamp, agent identity
- **Retention:** ~30 days
- **Access:** Query via Civic Chat (UI coming soon)
- **Output formats:** Aggregated summary, line-by-line with timestamps, CSV export (up to 5,000 lines)

Query examples in Civic Chat:
- "What did my agent do in the last hour?"
- "Show me all Gmail tool calls from yesterday"
- "Export last week's tool calls as CSV"

---

## Security: Revocation

Instant revocation — no delay, no waiting. Four levels of granularity:

1. **Block a single tool** — e.g., disable `send_gmail_message` while keeping the rest of Gmail
2. **Restrict server operations** — e.g., make an entire server read-only
3. **Block an entire server** — e.g., disable the Stripe server completely
4. **Revoke the entire toolkit** — cut off the agent from all tools at once

Revocation is accessible via Civic Chat or the app.civic.com UI. By design it is NOT accessible via the agent API — agents cannot self-revoke.

---

## Security: Secret Management

Civic stores credentials on behalf of developers and users:
- OAuth tokens (from OAuth flows)
- API keys and Bearer tokens (added manually)

**Core guarantee:** Secrets are never passed to or visible to the AI agent. The isolation flow:

```
AI Agent → Civic Hub (validates, applies guardrails, injects credential) → External service → Civic Hub → AI Agent (result only)
```

Secrets are added via the app.civic.com UI or via the Configurator Agent in Civic Chat ("Add my Stripe API key").

---

## Civic Chat (Control Plane)

Civic Chat at app.civic.com is the primary control plane — not a demo or test interface.

**Three specialized agents:**

1. **Configurator Agent** — Set up toolkits, guardrails, secrets via natural language. Example: "Create a support toolkit with Gmail and Slack, and add a guardrail to block PII in requests"
2. **Audit Agent** — Query audit logs. Example: "What did my agent do yesterday?" or "Export last week's tool calls as CSV"
3. **Revocation Agent** — Instantly revoke access. Example: "Block all Gmail writes" or "Revoke the entire support toolkit"

**Important restrictions:**
- Civic Chat only handles these three categories — configuration, audit, revocation
- Requests outside these tasks are blocked by design
- Consumes credits from the same pool as tool calls
- The Chat endpoint is protected and separated from the agent layer

---

## Billing

- **Free trial:** 10,000 credits, 14 days (whichever comes first)
- **Starter:** $25/month — 30,000 credits
- **Pro:** $99/month — 100,000 credits
- **Business:** $199/month — 450,000 credits

Credits are consumed by tool calls and Configurator Agent chat interactions.

---

# PART 2: CIVIC AUTH — USER AUTHENTICATION

## What is Civic Auth?

Civic Auth is a separate product for adding user authentication to web and mobile applications. Currently in beta.

**Features:**
- Email, Google, and wallet sign-in
- Optional embedded Web3 wallets (auto-created for users)
- Multichain: Solana, Base, Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, BSC, and other EVM chains

**Sign up:** https://auth.civic.com (get your Client ID in under a minute)

## Installation

```bash
npm install @civic/auth        # Web (React, Next.js, Node.js, Vanilla JS)
pip install civic-auth         # Python (FastAPI, Flask, Django)
npm install @civic/auth-web3   # Web3 wallet support
```

## Quick Integration (Next.js)

```typescript
// app/api/auth/[...civicauth]/route.ts
import { handler } from "@civic/auth/nextjs";
export const GET = handler;
export const POST = handler;
```

```typescript
// middleware.ts
import { authMiddleware } from "@civic/auth/nextjs";
export default authMiddleware();
export const config = { matcher: ["/((?!_next|favicon.ico).*)"] };
```

```tsx
// app/layout.tsx
import { CivicAuthProvider } from "@civic/auth/nextjs";

export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return (
    <html>
      <body>
        <CivicAuthProvider>{children}</CivicAuthProvider>
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

## Quick Integration (React)

```tsx
import { CivicAuthProvider, UserButton } from "@civic/auth/react";

function App() {
  return (
    <CivicAuthProvider clientId={process.env.REACT_APP_CIVIC_CLIENT_ID}>
      <UserButton />
    </CivicAuthProvider>
  );
}
```

## Quick Integration (Python / FastAPI)

```python
from fastapi import FastAPI
from civic_auth.integrations.fastapi import CivicAuth

app = FastAPI()
civic = CivicAuth(client_id=os.environ["CIVIC_CLIENT_ID"])

@app.get("/protected")
async def protected(user=Depends(civic.get_user)):
    return {"user": user}
```

## Framework Support

| Framework | Package | Guide |
|-----------|---------|-------|
| React | `@civic/auth/react` | https://docs.civic.com/integration/react |
| Next.js | `@civic/auth/nextjs` | https://docs.civic.com/integration/nextjs |
| Node.js | `@civic/auth` | https://docs.civic.com/integration/nodejs |
| Express | `@civic/auth` | https://docs.civic.com/integration/nodejs/express |
| Fastify | `@civic/auth` | https://docs.civic.com/integration/nodejs/fastify |
| Hono | `@civic/auth` | https://docs.civic.com/integration/nodejs/hono |
| FastAPI | `civic-auth` | https://docs.civic.com/integration/python/fastapi |
| Flask | `civic-auth` | https://docs.civic.com/integration/python/flask |
| Django | `civic-auth` | https://docs.civic.com/integration/python/django |
| React Native | `@civic/auth` | https://docs.civic.com/integration/mobile/react-native |
| Vanilla JS | `@civic/auth` | https://docs.civic.com/integration/vanillajs |
| Other (OIDC/OAuth2) | Any OIDC client | https://docs.civic.com/integration/other |

## Web3 Wallets

```typescript
import { CivicAuthProvider } from "@civic/auth-web3/react";
import { useWallet } from "@civic/auth-web3/react";

function App() {
  return (
    <CivicAuthProvider clientId="your-client-id">
      <WalletComponent />
    </CivicAuthProvider>
  );
}

function WalletComponent() {
  const { wallet } = useWallet();
  return <div>Address: {wallet?.address}</div>;
}
```

Supported chains: Solana, Ethereum, Base, Polygon, Arbitrum, BSC, and other EVM-compatible chains.

---

# PART 3: CIVIC LABS — EXPERIMENTAL PROJECTS

## What is Civic Labs?

Civic Labs is where Civic explores ideas in digital identity, agentic AI, and authorization. Projects are called "flasks" and are at various stages of development. All are open to community feedback and contribution.

**Focus areas:** Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools and AI security.

## Flask Projects

### MCP Hub
A hosted MCP Manager that unifies and orchestrates multiple MCP servers with a focus on auth and security. The production foundation for Civic Hub.
- Docs: https://docs.civic.com/labs/projects/mcp-hub

### Guardrail Proxy
Wrap any MCP server in a configurable, flexible security layer. Lets you add guardrails to MCP servers that don't natively support them.
- Docs: https://docs.civic.com/labs/projects/guardrail-proxy

### Bodyguard
LLM-based threat detection for prompts and tool calls. Detects prompt injection, jailbreak attempts, and other adversarial inputs before they reach your tools.
- Docs: https://docs.civic.com/labs/projects/bodyguard

### Pass-through Proxy
A middleware hook system for MCP servers. Powers guardrails and other cross-cutting concerns.
- Docs: https://docs.civic.com/labs/projects/passthrough-proxy

### Civic Knowledge
AI assistant for optimizing internal operations and processes. Demonstrates RAG over organizational knowledge.
- Docs: https://docs.civic.com/labs/projects/civic-knowledge

### x402-mcp
x402 payment protocol integration for MCP. Enables micropayments for agent tool calls.
- Docs: https://docs.civic.com/labs/projects/x402-mcp

## Labs Concepts

- **MCP**: What is MCP and why it matters for AI applications → https://docs.civic.com/labs/concepts/mcp
- **Guardrails**: Guardrails as a protection layer → https://docs.civic.com/labs/concepts/guardrails
- **Prompt Injection**: Understanding prompt injection attacks and LLM safety → https://docs.civic.com/labs/concepts/prompt-injection
- **Auth Strategies**: OAuth2, granular permissions, and consent flows → https://docs.civic.com/labs/concepts/auth-strategies
- **Hooks**: Middleware layer around MCP APIs → https://docs.civic.com/labs/concepts/hooks
- **RAG**: Retrieval strategies for LLMs → https://docs.civic.com/labs/concepts/rag

## Getting Started with Labs

Visit https://docs.civic.com/labs/private/getting-started to try out experimental projects.

To contribute or give feedback: https://docs.civic.com/labs/feedback

---

# KEY FACTS FOR AI AGENTS

## Most Common Integration Pattern

1. Sign up at https://app.civic.com
2. Complete onboarding (select framework + tools)
3. Generate a Civic token
4. Set env vars: `CIVIC_URL=https://app.civic.com/hub/mcp?profile=your-toolkit` and `CIVIC_TOKEN=your-token`
5. Connect using your framework's MCP client with `Authorization: Bearer $CIVIC_TOKEN`

## Critical Distinctions

- **Civic Hub** = the MCP gateway (core infrastructure)
- **Civic Chat** = the control plane UI at app.civic.com (configuration, audit, revocation)
- **Civic Auth** = separate user authentication product (not the same as Civic Hub)
- **Toolkit** = a set of MCP servers (switch between them, one active at a time)
- **Skill** = a set of instructions that layers ON TOP of the current toolkit
- **Profile locking** = default ON when `profile` param is set; prevents agents from modifying their own guardrails or switching toolkits

## Numbers to Know

- 95+ MCP servers
- ~200 tools available
- ~5,000 tool parameters
- ~1,000 server-specific guardrail rules (across 35 servers)
- 14 universal guardrails
- Free trial: 10,000 credits, 14 days
- Audit log retention: ~30 days

## Support

- Developer Slack: https://join.slack.com/t/civic-developers/shared_invite/zt-37tv9fyo7-aDT43mUjOFQwdQFmfZLTRw
- Troubleshooting: https://docs.civic.com/civic/troubleshooting
